checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE: Signature verification must be deterministic: for identical inputs (h,

reachability.notes: Different math libraries or constant-time vs non-constant-time implementations could yield different results for edge-case inputs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: Signature verification must be deterministic: for identical inputs (h, r, s, qx, qy), all implementations must produce identical output (either 32-byte success or empty).
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Non-deterministic verification causes consensus split across all clients processing the same block.

Test boundary values for state transitions affecting ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-CHECK-DATA-AVAILABLE: Data availability must be confirmed via is_data_available(hash_tree_ro

reachability.notes: Attacker withholds column sidecars or provides invalid KZG proofs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: Data availability must be confirmed via is_data_available(hash_tree_root(block)) before a block is accepted. This requires retrieving and verifying column sidecars.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Bypassing data availability checks allows blocks with unavailable data to be finalized, breaking the fundamental data availability guarantee of PeerDAS.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-SELECT-PEER-FOR-HISTORY: Before using a peer's advertised block range for history sync requests

validation_layers: Peer scoring based on serve behavior
reachability.notes: Malicious peers can claim false ranges to attract connections then fail to serve

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: Before using a peer's advertised block range for history sync requests, the node SHOULD verify that the peer actually serves the claimed range by requesting blocks. Peers that consistently fail to serve their advertised range MUST be downscored or disconnected according to AMB-EIP7642-02.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: False range claims could delay sync for individual nodes (eclipse-like behavior), but peer scoring should mitigate. Could affect network sync efficiency if widespread.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-SELECT-PEER-FOR-HISTORY with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths

subgraph.description: Models the sequential validation pipeline that data column sidecars must pass through when received via gossip, including structural checks, cryptographic verification, and fork-choice consistency checks.

nodes.label: Data Column Sidecar Received via Gossip
nodes.description: A data_column_sidecar message has been received on subnet data_column_sidecar_{subnet_id} but not yet validated

nodes.label: Verify Structural Validity
nodes.description: Run verify_data_column_sidecar(sidecar): check sidecar.index < NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS, len(kzg_commitments) > 0, len(kzg_commitments) <= max_blobs_per_block, and column/commitments/proofs length consistency

nodes.label: Verify Correct Subnet Routing
nodes.description: Check compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar(sidecar.index) == subnet_id to ensure the sidecar is on the correct gossip subnet

nodes.label: Check Slot Timeliness
nodes.description: Verify block_header.slot <= current_slot (with MAXIMUM_GOSSIP_CLOCK_DISPARITY allowance) to ignore future sidecars

nodes.label: Check Slot Above Finalization
nodes.description: Verify block_header.slot > compute_start_slot_at_epoch(state.finalized_checkpoint.epoch) to ignore already-finalized slots

nodes.label: Verify Proposer Signature
nodes.description: Validate the proposer signature on sidecar.signed_block_header

nodes.label: Check Parent Block Seen
nodes.description: Verify parent block has been seen via gossip or non-gossip sources; ignore if not

nodes.label: Verify Parent Passes Validation
nodes.description: Reject if parent block does not pass validation

nodes.label: Check Slot Higher Than Parent
nodes.description: Reject if sidecar slot is not strictly greater than parent slot

nodes.label: Check Finalized Checkpoint Ancestry
nodes.description: Verify get_checkpoint_block(store, parent_root, finalized_epoch) == finalized_root to ensure fork-choice consistency

nodes.label: Verify KZG Commitments Inclusion Proof
nodes.description: Run verify_data_column_sidecar_inclusion_proof: Merkle branch verification that kzg_commitments are included in block body_root at depth 4

nodes.label: Verify KZG Column Proofs
nodes.description: Run verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs: batch cell KZG proof verification of column data against commitments

nodes.label: Check Anti-Equivocation Uniqueness
nodes.description: Ignore if not the first valid sidecar for tuple (slot, proposer_index, column_index) with valid signature, inclusion proof, and KZG proof

nodes.label: Verify Expected Proposer Index
nodes.description: Reject if proposer_index does not match expected proposer for block slot in shuffling context defined by parent_root/slot. May queue if not immediately verifiable.

nodes.label: Data Column Sidecar Accepted
nodes.description: Sidecar has passed all gossip validation rules and is accepted for processing

nodes.label: Data Column Sidecar Rejected
nodes.description: Sidecar failed a REJECT validation rule and is discarded with potential peer penalty

nodes.label: Data Column Sidecar Ignored
nodes.description: Sidecar failed an IGNORE validation rule and is silently dropped without peer penalty

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-CONVERT-BLOBS-TO-DATA-COLUMNS: Throughout the distributed blob publishing flow, locally-produced data

reachability.notes: Deterministic erasure coding is critical for reconstruction

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: Throughout the distributed blob publishing flow, locally-produced data columns MUST maintain cryptographic consistency with the original blobs retrieved from the EL. If the EL is trusted (ASSUM-FULU-P2P-01 holds), erasure coding MUST be deterministic such that reconstruction yields original blobs.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-CONVERT-BLOBS-TO-DATA-COLUMNS'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Non-deterministic or incorrect erasure coding would break data reconstruction, causing DA failures affecting >50% of network. Even if EL is honest, buggy encoding breaks PeerDAS.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION: The Fulu fork transition must only occur at an epoch boundary where sl

reachability.notes: If the fork condition check is incorrect, nodes could fork at different epochs, causing a network split. This is an internal logic bug, not directly attacker-triggered.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: The Fulu fork transition must only occur at an epoch boundary where slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH == 0 AND compute_epoch_at_slot(slot) == FULU_FORK_EPOCH (411392).
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Incorrect fork activation epoch would split the entire network into incompatible chains.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-COMPUTE-WORDS: The multiplication complexity computation must use ceil(max_length / 8

reachability.notes: Floor vs ceil division produces different word counts for non-multiple-of-8 lengths, leading to different gas costs across clients.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: The multiplication complexity computation must use ceil(max_length / 8) for word calculation, not floor or truncating division. Rounding differences would produce different gas costs.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Rounding difference in word computation causes gas cost divergence between clients, leading to consensus split.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-COMPUTE-WORDS with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-VERIFY-COLUMN-PROOFS: The count of KZG-verified columns MUST monotonically increase during t

reachability.notes: If verified column count can decrease (e.g., due to re-verification bugs), the node could oscillate between available/unavailable states.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: The count of KZG-verified columns MUST monotonically increase during the sampling process within a single slot.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-VERIFY-COLUMN-PROOFS'.

4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Incorrect column counting could cause individual nodes to make wrong DA decisions but unlikely to affect >33% of network.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition on STATE-RECONSTRUCTION-FAILED: When reconstruction fails due to insufficient columns, the node MUST c

reachability.notes: Attacker withholding columns could trigger this path; node must not use partial data.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: When reconstruction fails due to insufficient columns, the node MUST correctly report data unavailability and NOT attempt to use partial data.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.
3. After each execution, verify:
   a. All expected side-effects occurred correctly
   b. No unexpected state changes occurred
   c. Return values match the post-condition specification
4. Test with boundary inputs that are valid but extreme.
5. Check partial execution/failure scenarios.

subgraph.title: Fulu P2P: Data Column Sidecar Request/Response Protocol
subgraph.description: Models the request/response networking protocols for retrieving data column sidecars (by range and by root), including serve range calculation, response ordering, and error handling.

nodes.label: DataColumnSidecarsByRange Request Received
nodes.description: Node receives a DataColumnSidecarsByRange request with (start_slot, count, columns) parameters

nodes.label: Compute Data Column Serve Range
nodes.description: Calculate data_column_serve_range = [max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH), current_epoch]

nodes.label: Check Request Within Serve Range
nodes.description: Verify the requested slot range overlaps with the data_column_serve_range

nodes.label: Retrieve Data Columns for Range
nodes.description: Fetch data column sidecars from local storage for requested (slot, column_index) combinations within the serve range

nodes.label: Order Response by (slot, column_index)
nodes.description: Sort response sidecars in ascending (slot, column_index) order as required by protocol

nodes.label: Range Response Streamed
nodes.description: Response chunks have been streamed back to requesting peer in correct order

nodes.label: DataColumnSidecarsByRoot Request Received
nodes.description: Node receives a DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request with list of (block_root, columns) identifiers

nodes.label: Compute Minimum Request Epoch
nodes.description: Calculate minimum_request_epoch = max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH)

nodes.label: Check Block Epoch Eligibility
nodes.description: For each block_root, verify the block epoch >= minimum_request_epoch

nodes.label: ResourceUnavailable Error Sent
nodes.description: Error code 3 (ResourceUnavailable) returned for blocks earlier than minimum_request_epoch

nodes.label: Retrieve Data Columns by Root
nodes.description: Fetch data column sidecars from local storage for the requested block roots and column indices

nodes.label: Ensure Response Passes Gossip Validation
nodes.description: Only respond with sidecars that pass gossip validation rules; never send sidecars from blocks failing gossip or state transition validation

nodes.label: Root Response Streamed
nodes.description: Valid data column sidecars streamed back to requesting peer

nodes.label: Enforce MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS
nodes.description: Ensure no more than MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS items are returned in a single response

edges.label: Determine what range this node can serve
Compare requested range against serve range
Request entirely outside serve range → error
Request within serve range → retrieve columns
Check each block root against minimum epoch
Block too old → ResourceUnavailable error code 3
Verify retrieved sidecars pass gossip validation
